job_title: Data Analyst for Ambulatory Pharmacy Operations
strengths: Proficient in Power BI/Tableau and dashboarding
ETL and pipeline building with BigQuery/Snowflake
Quantified impact in prior roles (e.g., 40% efficiency)
weaknesses: Limited or no explicit healthcare/pharmacy experience
No EPIC or pharmacy dispensing system experience listed
Limited explicit revenue cycle or reimbursement analysis
No direct mention of clinical KPIs like PDC/MPR
improvement_bullets: Optimized data integration pipelines for CRM and POS systems, utilizing techniques transferable to clinical workflows like EHR (Epic) integration and payer portal data mapping to ensure revenue cycle accuracy.

Engineered Python-based reporting models to track operational KPIs, including turnaround times and adherence metrics, ensuring data integrity for high-stakes business and financial performance monitoring.
Analyzed complex datasets to identify process bottlenecks, applying root-cause analysis similar to those required for pharmacy inventory management and clinical intervention quality initiatives.

Standardized organizational KPIs and data governance frameworks, supporting stakeholders in aligning with industry compliance standards akin to URAC/ACHC specialty pharmacy accreditation requirements.
Conducted financial impact modeling and segmentation analysis, enabling leadership to forecast revenue trends and optimize resource allocation across diverse business lines.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Data Analyst for Ambulatory Pharmacy Operations opportunity with Westchester Medical Center Health Network. I am interested in this opportunity because of WMCHealth’s commitment to advancing clinical outcomes through data-driven operational excellence. What interests me about working for WMCHealth is its reputation for integrating complex ambulatory pharmacy services within a large-scale hospital system, which intrigues me because of the potential to bridge the gap between technical data engineering and improved patient care. I believe my advanced data pipeline architecture skills and my ability to translate complex statistical findings into actionable financial strategy would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by automating data ingestion to ensure a 'single source of truth' for business stakeholders. In fact, at Shine Masters, I built a Python-driven reporting suite that ingested disparate CRM and POS data to replace manual entry; this transition not only reduced reporting discrepancies but also provided leadership with real-time visibility into high-value customer segments, resulting in a 300% revenue increase in that segment. Ultimately, I know that the Data Analyst for Ambulatory Pharmacy Operations role at WMCHealth would allow me to bring my data modeling expertise to pharmacy performance and financial integrity initiatives. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst & Operations Lead enabled me to develop my cross-functional collaboration skills. I led the standardization of organizational KPIs and data governance across the company, effectively closing the gap between raw data and growth strategy. By translating complex technical metrics into accessible reports for stakeholders, I empowered the team to make faster, evidence-based decisions, an approach I am eager to apply to clinical quality initiatives and pharmacy-led care services. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my background in SQL and Power BI in working on ambulatory pharmacy performance and clinical program outcomes at WMCHealth. I know that my expertise in building robust data pipelines and my focus on operational optimization align with what it takes to succeed at the Data Analyst for Ambulatory Pharmacy Operations role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
